content.problemStatement:
Write a program to input two student's names and percentages and print the name of the student with the higher percentage. In case they score equal percentages, print '**equal**'.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two strings: the name of the first student and the name of the second student.
The second line of input will contain two percentages: the percentage of the first student and the percentage of the second student.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two strings denoting the names of the two students (without spaces). The second line contains two integers denoting their percentages.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the name of the student with the higher percentage. If both percentages are equal, output **equal**.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only one test case. Amit's percentage (95) is higher than Swarup's (92), so Amit is printed.
Doe's percentage (95) exceeds John's (92), therefore Doe is printed.
Both percentages are equal (95), so the special token **equal** is printed.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.input:
4
Riya Karan
85 85
Mona Lisa
99 100
Bob Alice
45 44
Zed Yara
0 100

solution.approach: For each test case we simply compare the two integer percentages. If the first percentage is larger we output the first name, if the second is larger we output the second name, otherwise the percentages are equal and we output the special token **equal**. The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case and uses only a few scalar variables, making it optimal for the given constraints.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case
